We live in Hudson, but we evacuated.
We've been checking our ring cameras while we still have power to do so.
Watched our gutter get torn off the roof and fly into the woods.
Then checked again and saw that our back door blew open. It was banging wildly, letting wind and rain into our house. We called every neighbor we could think of to see if anyone stayed behind, one neighbor from our Street did not evacuate and he went and secured our home before major damage or absolute loss could occur.
Feeling enormous gratitude right now, but it's not over yet.
Stay safe and good luck to all in Milton's path!

Say this out loud, all who is reading this.
Heavenly Father,
We come before You today, standing in the gap for Florida, a state beloved by many. We acknowledge Your sovereignty over the heavens and the earth, over the winds and the waters. You, who once commanded the storm to be still, we invoke Your divine authority now.
Lord, protect Florida and its inhabitants. Shield them under Your mighty wings. Let Your peace that surpasses all understanding guard their hearts and minds in Christ Jesus. Provide comfort for the anxious, strength for the weary, and hope for the despairing.
Lord Jesus, who walked on water and calmed the sea, we ask that You command the hurricane approaching Florida to dissipate. In the name of Jesus Christ, we say to this storm: Be still! Let the winds die down, the rains cease, and the threat of destruction be no more. Let Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven, turning this potential for chaos into a display of Your mercy and power.
Father, should any harm come, we pray for swift recovery. Send Your Spirit to inspire acts of kindness, unity, and rapid restoration. Let communities come together, not just in survival, but in revival, showing the world what it means to be Your hands and feet.
In the name of Jesus Christ, we declare this storm's path altered, its strength diminished, and its harm minimized. We trust in Your Word, which says mountains can be moved with faith the size of a mustard seed.
We thank You, Lord, for Your unending love and for answering us when we call upon Your name. Let this prayer not only be a plea for safety but a testament to Your glory, showing that through Christ, even nature bows.
In Jesus' name, we pray.
